prose:
**Fukui reactivity indices for furan** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier standard, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied (furan.xyz). Fukui indices computed as f⁺ = q(N−1) − q(N) and f⁻ = q(N) − q(N+1) where q denotes Mulliken atomic charges, with neutral (N), cation N+1 (mult. 2), and anion N−1 (mult. 2) states all optimized at the same level. SCF converged for all three states. Charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0, Σf⁻ = 1.0, indicating well-behaved N±1 calculations). 

**Most electrophilic site:** C4 (β-carbon, C atom index 4) with f⁺ = 1.9243 (most prone to electrophilic attack). **Most nucleophilic site:** also C4 with f⁻ = 0.1616. The **dual descriptor** (f⁺ − f⁻) shows C4/C5 (β-carbons) are electrophile-preferential (positive: +1.763), while oxygen (f⁺ − f⁻ = −1.415) and hydrogens are nucleophile-preferential (negative). Full atomic-resolved Fukui indices, Mulliken charges (neutral state), and a bar chart are in the results above. Plot saved to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260704-052815_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. No warnings or convergence issues reported; integrity status = ok.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">9
